title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers player outcomes, repository baseline, accessibility, and evidence into a changed player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 3659 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Player Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Player Outcome, keep Player Outcome connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

A recognizable triangle and rocks constitute a complete game outcome. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Evidence Ladder · changed case · Lab Evidence Ladder, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A lab Asteroids team handles case 1849 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

options.text: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.
A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible. That claim does not survive the evidence ladder · changed case evidence boundary.

For evidence ladder · changed case, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-8h8n4l" that uses case 9811 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-8h8n4l
# Competency: asteroids-evidence-ladder.
# Case 9811: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_evidence_ladder_8h8n4l(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_8h8n4l = "8h8n4l-l4n8h8-9811"
    assert evidence_variant_8h8n4l
    evidence_axes_8h8n4l = (
        "player8h8n4l",
        "seed9811",
        "inputl4n8h8",
        "frame8h8n4l9811",
        "state98118h8n4l",
        "playtestl4n8h89811",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_8h8n4l)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 7611 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
